properties.descricao: The memorandum of understanding is scheduled to be officially signed on Friday in Switzerland.

properties.titulo_evento: US strike kills leader of Venezuela’s Tren de Aragua gang, White House says

properties.titulo_evento: Ukraine: Kyiv under fire again as russian strikes send residents into shelters
properties.descricao: Ukraine's capital came under a major missile attack early on 15 June, with air defence systems intercepting projectiles above the city. Authorities reported injuries as residents sought refuge in shelters and metro stations.

properties.titulo_evento: Switzerland: Clashes in Geneva as 20,000 protest G7 summit and global policies
properties.descricao: Clashes broke out in Geneva on 14 June after a large demonstration held ahead of the G7 summit in nearby Évian-les-Bains, France, turned violent near the United Nations district.

properties.titulo_evento: Germany cruises past Curaçao 7-1 and Netherlands-Japan tie 2-2 as World Cup group stages continue

properties.titulo_evento: French fans flood New York ahead of world Cup opener

properties.titulo_evento: Strait of Hormuz reopening won't restore normal prewar shipping any time soon
properties.descricao: A proposed US-Iran deal to reopen the Strait of Hormuz is raising hopes for global shipping and oil markets. But mines, high insurance costs and geopolitical risks mean disruption could persist for months.

properties.titulo_evento: Ibrahim Maza: Algeria's 'multicultural kid' from Berlin
properties.descricao: Ibo Maza had his choice of three different countries. Instead of his native Germany, the Bayer Leverkusen forward is representing Algeria, the country of his father, at the World Cup in North America.

properties.titulo_evento: US-Iran: Reported peace deal sparks both relief and anger
properties.descricao: A peace deal between the US and Iran is due to be signed this week — and while the hopes of peace may yet be dashed, the news alone was enough to trigger celebrations, backlash and political infighting on both sides.

properties.titulo_evento: Iran war: Will the global energy crisis end soon?
properties.descricao: A potential US-Iran deal to end the war and reopen the Strait of Hormuz could ease the global energy crunch, but oil prices and supplies may take months to stabilize as shipping restarts and infrastructure recovers.

properties.titulo_evento: G7: Leaders gather to grapple with wars, global economy
properties.descricao: The heads of the world's richest nations gather in France as the wars in Iran and Ukraine dominate the global agenda. The US has promised peace in Iran; fellow leaders are cautiously optimistic. DW has the latest.

properties.titulo_evento: Russia strikes leave historic Kyiv cathedral in flames
properties.descricao: The Kyiv Pechersk Lavra monastery, a UNESCO World Heritage Site, was set on fire as Russia struck multiple cities in Ukraine overnight. Meanwhile, Moscow reported three deaths from a Ukrainian drone attack.

properties.titulo_evento: Trump hosts White House cage fights to mark 80th birthday
properties.descricao: The US president watched seven fights with an audience of 4,000 people inside a specially built arena on the White House lawn. Critics say the unprecendented spectacle is in poor taste amid the ongoing Iran war.

properties.titulo_evento: Iran war: US, Iran announce tentative peace deal
properties.descricao: Pakistani PM Shehbaz Sharif has announced a peace deal between the US and Iran, with a signing ceremony on June 19. Sharif said both sides would terminate military operations, including in Lebanon. DW has more.

properties.titulo_evento: UK seizes suspected Russian shadow fleet tanker

properties.titulo_evento: Manuel Neuer fit to join Germany in first World Cup match
properties.descricao: Veteran goalkeeper Manuel Neuer is set to play in Germany's first match in this year's World Cup. He had retired from the national team in August 2024.

properties.titulo_evento: Germany news: President Steinmeier heads to Asia
